props.title: What Stripe Built in 2014 and AI Tools Are Only Now Getting Around To

props.text: Webhook delivery logs and redelivery buttons have been standard operations furniture in payments and developer infrastructure for over a decade — Stripe's Event deliveries tab, GitHub's Recent deliveries page with its 3-day retention and Redeliver button, exposed as a REST API since June 2021. The AI agent ecosystem has spent its observability budget on reasoning traces and tool-use visualization while leaving the outbound side mostly opaque: OpenAI webhooks only on async jobs, Anthropic with no native surface, Gemini just adding them in 2026. PR #73 ships the Stripe-style delivery log to MiroShark's completion webhook — JSONL log per sim with masked URLs, status code and latency per attempt, an admin-gated retry endpoint with a `retry: true` payload marker, and a panel in EmbedDialog with the boring buttons every operator actually opens.
